channel.title: Finance Pulse
channel.description: A daily banking finance-function intelligence briefing: macro, banking, regulatory, and AI in finance, built for client conversations.
channel.itunes:author: Shawn Mascia
channel.itunes:summary: A daily banking finance-function intelligence briefing: macro, banking, regulatory, and AI in finance, built for client conversations.

item.title: Finance Pulse - Jun 4, 2026
item.description:
Now I have enough data to compose both parts. Let me compile the briefing.
---
# Finance Pulse | Thursday, June 4, 2026
**Bottom line: The Fed is frozen at three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent amid Middle East-driven inflation, super-regionals are running on commercial lending and fee momentum, Basel III Endgame "take two" is entering its final comment window on June eighteenth, and agentic AI is crossing from pilot to production across the finance function with vendor consolidation accelerating.**
---
## Top Takeaways
1. April 2026 CPI rose to three-point-eight percent year-over-year, driven by energy price spikes from Middle East tensions. With the funds rate held at three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent and unemployment at four-point-three to four-point-four percent, markets are pricing in zero cuts in 2026 at a sixty-eight-point-eight percent probability.
2. Q1 earnings from PNC, KeyCorp, U.S. Bancorp, Truist, Fifth Third, and Regions show commercial lending gaining momentum, fee-based businesses carrying more weight, and technology investments beginning to reshape how banks compete.
3. The March 2026 Basel III re-proposal represents a directional shift from the 2023 draft, which had called for significantly increased capital requirements. The agencies now note the new proposals would "modestly reduce capital requirements for large banks and moderately reduce requirements for smaller banks."
4. OneStream reported AI bookings and customers more than doubled in 2025, with its SensibleAI improving forecasting accuracy by twenty-seven percent on average and accelerating planning cycles by eighty-six percent on average.
5. FIS announced its Financial Crimes AI Agent, built with Anthropic, to compress AML alert and case investigations from days to minutes, with BMO and Amalgamated Bank in development and general availability planned for second-half 2026.
---
## Key Themes
### 1. "Higher-for-Longer Bites Into ALM Planning" | **Evolving**
The Fed held the funds rate unchanged at the three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent target range for a third consecutive meeting in April 2026. The decision was not unanimous: the eight-to-four vote marked the first time since October 1992 that four officials dissented against an FOMC decision. J.P. Morgan Global Research sees the Fed holding rates steady for the rest of 2026, with the next move likely being a hike of twenty-five basis points in the third quarter of 2027. For CFOs and treasurers, this narrows the ALM playbook: asset repricing is slowing while liability repricing has largely run its course.
### 2. "Commercial Lending and Fees Replacing Consumer NII Tailwind" | **Recurring**
The clearest change across the super-regional group in Q1 was in commercial lending behavior: borrowers are not only taking out new loans but also drawing more heavily on existing lines. At Regions, CFO Anil Chadha told analysts: "Approximately half [of loan growth] was driven by higher line utilization, with the remainder from new loan originations, primarily to existing clients." U.S. Bancorp highlighted sustained fee-based growth tied to merchant services and embedded payment tools; KeyCorp reported a twelve percent increase in priority fee-based businesses including commercial payments and investment banking.
### 3. "Agentic AI Moves from Pilot to Production in Finance Functions" | **New**
According to Wolters Kluwer, forty-four percent of finance teams will use agentic AI in 2026, representing an increase of over six hundred percent. Yet ninety-nine percent of companies plan to put agents into production while only eleven percent have done so, due to implementation challenges related to data, governance, and security. The gap between intent and deployment is the transformation consultant's primary opening.
---
## Banking Finance-Function
NIM in 2026 will depend on whether the Fed holds or cuts, but with stable rates and continued deposit repricing the outlook is for modest further expansion or flat margins. 2026 may be the first year since the rate-hike cycle where year-over-year NIM comparisons are relatively flat.
**Truist (Q1 2026):** Net interest income of three-point-six billion dollars in Q1 versus three-point-seven billion in Q4 2025, with noninterest income stable at one-point-five-five billion.
**Fifth Third:** Completed its merger with Comerica on February 2, 2026, creating the ninth-largest U.S. bank with approximately two hundred ninety-four billion dollars in assets. Fifth Third reported a CET1 ratio of eleven-point-two percent in Q1 2026, above peers.
**PNC (Q1 2026):** Net interest income of three-point-nine-six billion dollars in Q1 2026, up from three-point-seven-three billion in Q4 2025 and three-point-four-eight billion a year ago — a strong NII trajectory driven by commercial mix shift and deposit repricing.
TransUnion projects credit card delinquency rates will remain virtually flat, with the ninety-plus-days-past-due rate forecast to rise by just one basis point to two-point-five-seven percent in 2026, providing a credit quality tailwind for provisioning discussions.
---
## Regulatory Radar
**Basel III Endgame Re-Proposal:** On March nineteenth, 2026, regulators unveiled a revised proposal recalibrating the 2023 framework. Fed Vice Chair Michelle Bowman indicated the update seeks to remove overlapping requirements and better align capital with underlying risk. Comments are due by June eighteenth, 2026. The March 2026 proposals revisit Basel III Endgame for the largest firms, introduce a separate approach for regional and smaller banks, and revise the GSIB surcharge framework. The package creates new strategic and operational questions for treasury, risk, finance, reporting, and data teams as firms assess impact and prepare for implementation.
**DFAST 2026:** The 2026 supervisory stress test scenarios start in the first quarter of 2026 and extend through the first quarter of 2029, covering twenty-eight variables identical to the 2025 set. Covered institutions must publish a summary of results in the period starting June fifteenth and ending July fifteenth. Finance teams should be in active Stress Capital Buffer scenario analysis mode now.
---
## AI in Finance
**Agentic AI: Real Deployments vs. Vendor Marketing**
Finance teams sit on a unique stack of structured data, recurring cycles, and material decisions, making the function one of the highest-ROI surfaces for agentic AI when the controls hold. The playbook spans FP&A augmentation, close acceleration, variance analysis, and scenario modeling.
The critical real-versus-hype test: Most platforms marketed as "AI for FP&A" do not have true agentic architecture. They have AI-generated commentary on top of a planning interface. That is a meaningful improvement over a blank spreadsheet. It is not agentic finance.
**OneStream / Hg acquisition:** OneStream's Finance AI differentiation brings AI and Agentic AI solutions to a company's contextualized business logic. Hg, which has scaled portfolio companies to an aggregate enterprise value exceeding one hundred eighty-five billion dollars, will back accelerated innovation.
**FIS + Anthropic:** FIS announced on May fourth that it is working with Anthropic to bring agentic AI to banking, beginning with the Financial Crimes AI Agent. BMO and Amalgamated Bank will be among the first institutions to deploy the agent.
**Oracle Financial Services:** Oracle Financial Services announced an enterprise-class suite of AI-infused applications, design tools, frameworks, and pre-built AI agents at the Oracle Financial Services Summit in New York in February 2026.
**Workforce signal:** A new "finance agent owner" role is emerging in mature programs, sitting under the controller or FP&A lead and responsible for prompt curation, evaluation maintenance, and the evidence pipeline. This role does not exist in traditional finance org charts; teams that absorb the work into existing roles often see it deprioritized under cycle pressure.
---
## CFO Agenda, FP&A, and Transformation Signals
- CFOs who spent the last few years watching vendors bolt AI labels onto legacy features are now seeing a different picture: a generation of platforms that have rebuilt core finance processes around machine learning, with measurable outcomes in areas like autonomous close, agentic treasury, and AI-driven financial reporting. - The AFP Treasury Benchmarking Survey found that organizations using AI-assisted treasury and cash flow tools achieve eighty-eight to ninety-two percent forecast accuracy at a thirteen-week horizon, compared to sixty percent for organizations using manual or semi-automated methods. - Banks have approximately two years to interpret the new Basel III capital rules, assess their impact, address new data and technology needs, and adjust business strategies. Basel III Endgame is a chance to modernize capital infrastructure by updating technology, becoming more agile, and addressing inefficiencies to lower operating costs. - CFOs must communicate AI's staged ROI. First-half 2026 results may reflect increased AI spend or pilot costs; CFOs should clarify longer payoff horizons.
---
## Contrarian Insight
The super-regional efficiency narrative risks being hijacked by AI capital expenditure. Ongoing cloud migration and AI integration are set to drive operational efficiencies, but management at several banks has acknowledged that technology expenses will remain elevated over the next eighteen to twenty-four months before stabilizing. McKinsey projects AI could reduce certain cost categories by as much as seventy percent across the banking industry, but the net effect is expected to be fifteen to twenty percent, or seven hundred to eight hundred billion dollars, due to rising AI technology costs. Selling boards on efficiency ratios improving before technology spend peaks is the real transformation CFO challenge of the next six quarters.
---
## Client Conversation Hooks
1. **The June eighteenth comment deadline on Basel III:** Has your client's capital and treasury team stress-tested the re-proposal's standardized-approach changes against their specific loan book and GSIB surcharge exposure? The window to shape the final rule is closing.
2. **The "finance agent owner" role:** Ask your client's controller or FP&A lead: who owns the audit trail when an AI agent posts a journal or flags a reconciliation anomaly? That unnamed role gap is where transformation programs fail at the SOX sign-off stage.
3. **PNC's NII trajectory vs. Truist's sequential decline:** NII rose at PNC while slipping sequentially at Truist in Q1. The divergence is almost entirely deposit mix and commercial repricing discipline. That conversation is the ideal entry point into a deposit-cost benchmarking and FTP modernization engagement.
---
item.itunes:summary:
Now I have enough data to compose both parts. Let me compile the briefing.
---
# Finance Pulse | Thursday, June 4, 2026
**Bottom line: The Fed is frozen at three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent amid Middle East-driven inflation, super-regionals are running on commercial lending and fee momentum, Basel III Endgame "take two" is entering its final comment window on June eighteenth, and agentic AI is crossing from pilot to production across the finance function with vendor consolidation accelerating.**
---
## Top Takeaways
1. April 2026 CPI rose to three-point-eight percent year-over-year, driven by energy price spikes from Middle East tensions. With the funds rate held at three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent and unemployment at four-point-three to four-point-four percent, markets are pricing in zero cuts in 2026 at a sixty-eight-point-eight percent probability.
2. Q1 earnings from PNC, KeyCorp, U.S. Bancorp, Truist, Fifth Third, and Regions show commercial lending gaining momentum, fee-based businesses carrying more weight, and technology investments beginning to reshape how banks compete.
3. The March 2026 Basel III re-proposal represents a directional shift from the 2023 draft, which had called for significantly increased capital requirements. The agencies now note the new proposals would "modestly reduce capital requirements for large banks and moderately reduce requirements for smaller banks."
4. OneStream reported AI bookings and customers more than doubled in 2025, with its SensibleAI improving forecasting accuracy by twenty-seven percent on average and accelerating planning cycles by eighty-six percent on average.
5. FIS announced its Financial Crimes AI Agent, built with Anthropic, to compress AML alert and case investigations from days to minutes, with BMO and Amalgamated Bank in development and general availability planned for second-half 2026.
---
## Key Themes
### 1. "Higher-for-Longer Bites Into ALM Planning" | **Evolving**
The Fed held the funds rate unchanged at the three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent target range for a third consecutive meeting in April 2026. The decision was not unanimous: the eight-to-four vote marked the first time since October 1992 that four officials dissented against an FOMC decision. J.P. Morgan Global Research sees the Fed holding rates steady for the rest of 2026, with the next move likely being a hike of twenty-five basis points in the third quarter of 2027. For CFOs and treasurers, this narrows the ALM playbook: asset repricing is slowing while liability repricing has largely run its course.
### 2. "Commercial Lending and Fees Replacing Consumer NII Tailwind" | **Recurring**
The clearest change across the super-regional group in Q1 was in commercial lending behavior: borrowers are not only taking out new loans but also drawing more heavily on existing lines. At Regions, CFO Anil Chadha told analysts: "Approximately half [of loan growth] was driven by higher line utilization, with the remainder from new loan originations, primarily to existing clients." U.S. Bancorp highlighted sustained fee-based growth tied to merchant services and embedded payment tools; KeyCorp reported a twelve percent increase in priority fee-based businesses including commercial payments and investment banking.
### 3. "Agentic AI Moves from Pilot to Production in Finance Functions" | **New**
According to Wolters Kluwer, forty-four percent of finance teams will use agentic AI in 2026, representing an increase of over six hundred percent. Yet ninety-nine percent of companies plan to put agents into production while only eleven percent have done so, due to implementation challenges related to data, governance, and security. The gap between intent and deployment is the transformation consultant's primary opening.
---
## Banking Finance-Function
NIM in 2026 will depend on whether the Fed holds or cuts, but with stable rates and continued deposit repricing the outlook is for modest further expansion or flat margins. 2026 may be the first year since the rate-hike cycle where year-over-year NIM comparisons are relatively flat.
**Truist (Q1 2026):** Net interest income of three-point-six billion dollars in Q1 versus three-point-seven billion in Q4 2025, with noninterest income stable at one-point-five-five billion.
**Fifth Third:** Completed its merger with Comerica on February 2, 2026, creating the ninth-largest U.S. bank with approximately two hundred ninety-four billion dollars in assets. Fifth Third reported a CET1 ratio of eleven-point-two percent in Q1 2026, above peers.
**PNC (Q1 2026):** Net interest income of three-point-nine-six billion dollars in Q1 2026, up from three-point-seven-three billion in Q4 2025 and three-point-four-eight billion a year ago — a strong NII trajectory driven by commercial mix shift and deposit repricing.
TransUnion projects credit card delinquency rates will remain virtually flat, with the ninety-plus-days-past-due rate forecast to rise by just one basis point to two-point-five-seven percent in 2026, providing a credit quality tailwind for provisioning discussions.
---
## Regulatory Radar
**Basel III Endgame Re-Proposal:** On March nineteenth, 2026, regulators unveiled a revised proposal recalibrating the 2023 framework. Fed Vice Chair Michelle Bowman indicated the update seeks to remove overlapping requirements and better align capital with underlying risk. Comments are due by June eighteenth, 2026. The March 2026 proposals revisit Basel III Endgame for the largest firms, introduce a separate approach for regional and smaller banks, and revise the GSIB surcharge framework. The package creates new strategic and operational questions for treasury, risk, finance, reporting, and data teams as firms assess impact and prepare for implementation.
**DFAST 2026:** The 2026 supervisory stress test scenarios start in the first quarter of 2026 and extend through the first quarter of 2029, covering twenty-eight variables identical to the 2025 set. Covered institutions must publish a summary of results in the period starting June fifteenth and ending July fifteenth. Finance teams should be in active Stress Capital Buffer scenario analysis mode now.
---
## AI in Finance
**Agentic AI: Real Deployments vs. Vendor Marketing**
Finance teams sit on a unique stack of structured data, recurring cycles, and material decisions, making the function one of the highest-ROI surfaces for agentic AI when the controls hold. The playbook spans FP&A augmentation, close acceleration, variance analysis, and scenario modeling.
The critical real-versus-hype test: Most platforms marketed as "AI for FP&A" do not have true agentic architecture. They have AI-generated commentary on top of a planning interface. That is a meaningful improvement over a blank spreadsheet. It is not agentic finance.
**OneStream / Hg acquisition:** OneStream's Finance AI differentiation brings AI and Agentic AI solutions to a company's contextualized business logic. Hg, which has scaled portfolio companies to an aggregate enterprise value exceeding one hundred eighty-five billion dollars, will back accelerated innovation.
**FIS + Anthropic:** FIS announced on May fourth that it is working with Anthropic to bring agentic AI to banking, beginning with the Financial Crimes AI Agent. BMO and Amalgamated Bank will be among the first institutions to deploy the agent.
**Oracle Financial Services:** Oracle Financial Services announced an enterprise-class suite of AI-infused applications, design tools, frameworks, and pre-built AI agents at the Oracle Financial Services Summit in New York in February 2026.
**Workforce signal:** A new "finance agent owner" role is emerging in mature programs, sitting under the controller or FP&A lead and responsible for prompt curation, evaluation maintenance, and the evidence pipeline. This role does not exist in traditional finance org charts; teams that absorb the work into existing roles often see it deprioritized under cycle pressure.
---
## CFO Agenda, FP&A, and Transformation Signals
- CFOs who spent the last few years watching vendors bolt AI labels onto legacy features are now seeing a different picture: a generation of platforms that have rebuilt core finance processes around machine learning, with measurable outcomes in areas like autonomous close, agentic treasury, and AI-driven financial reporting. - The AFP Treasury Benchmarking Survey found that organizations using AI-assisted treasury and cash flow tools achieve eighty-eight to ninety-two percent forecast accuracy at a thirteen-week horizon, compared to sixty percent for organizations using manual or semi-automated methods. - Banks have approximately two years to interpret the new Basel III capital rules, assess their impact, address new data and technology needs, and adjust business strategies. Basel III Endgame is a chance to modernize capital infrastructure by updating technology, becoming more agile, and addressing inefficiencies to lower operating costs. - CFOs must communicate AI's staged ROI. First-half 2026 results may reflect increased AI spend or pilot costs; CFOs should clarify longer payoff horizons.
---
## Contrarian Insight
The super-regional efficiency narrative risks being hijacked by AI capital expenditure. Ongoing cloud migration and AI integration are set to drive operational efficiencies, but management at several banks has acknowledged that technology expenses will remain elevated over the next eighteen to twenty-four months before stabilizing. McKinsey projects AI could reduce certain cost categories by as much as seventy percent across the banking industry, but the net effect is expected to be fifteen to twenty percent, or seven hundred to eight hundred billion dollars, due to rising AI technology costs. Selling boards on efficiency ratios improving before technology spend peaks is the real transformation CFO challenge of the next six quarters.
---
## Client Conversation Hooks
1. **The June eighteenth comment deadline on Basel III:** Has your client's capital and treasury team stress-tested the re-proposal's standardized-approach changes against their specific loan book and GSIB surcharge exposure? The window to shape the final rule is closing.
2. **The "finance agent owner" role:** Ask your client's controller or FP&A lead: who owns the audit trail when an AI agent posts a journal or flags a reconciliation anomaly? That unnamed role gap is where transformation programs fail at the SOX sign-off stage.
3. **PNC's NII trajectory vs. Truist's sequential decline:** NII rose at PNC while slipping sequentially at Truist in Q1. The divergence is almost entirely deposit mix and commercial repricing discipline. That conversation is the ideal entry point into a deposit-cost benchmarking and FTP modernization engagement.
---

item.title: Finance Pulse - Jun 3, 2026
item.description:
Now I have strong signal across all required coverage areas. Let me compile both outputs.
---
# Finance Pulse | Wednesday, June 3, 2026
**Bottom line: With the Fed locked at three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent and geopolitical inflation risks tilting the next move toward a hike, super-regional bank CFOs face a higher-for-longer rate world that is simultaneously protecting NIM stability, pressuring credit-line utilization modeling, and compressing the timeline for Basel III Endgame comment responses due June 18.**
---
## Top Takeaways
1. The Fed held the federal funds rate unchanged at the three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent target range for a third consecutive meeting in April 2026. BofA Global Research now expects the Fed to remain on hold for the rest of this year, with two quarter-point cuts pushed all the way to July and September 2027.
2. The FDIC, OCC, and Federal Reserve released proposed regulations to revise U.S. bank capital rules that would generally ease capital requirements, with comments on all three proposals due by June 18, 2026.
3. First-quarter earnings from PNC, KeyCorp, U.S. Bancorp, Truist, Fifth Third, and Regions show commercial lending gaining momentum, fee-based businesses carrying more weight, and technology investments beginning to reshape how banks compete for client relationships.
4. Bain research finds that roughly twelve percent of finance organizations have deployed machine learning in FP&A forecasting at full scale. Yet in many cases, the underlying process has not changed, with AI-generated forecasts running alongside existing bottom-up planning cycles: two processes running in parallel, neither fully trusted.
5. BlackLine CEO Owen Ryan reported accelerating revenue growth in Q1 2026 and declared that the company is "defining the future of the financial close with Agentic Financial Operations," with the growing adoption of Verity AI and Studio360 demonstrating that CFOs view BlackLine as the "essential governance layer for the AI era."
---
## Key Themes
### 1. Rate Plateau Becomes the Planning Baseline **[Recurring]**
A majority of Fed officials highlighted that some policy firming would likely become appropriate if inflation were to continue to run persistently above two percent, according to the FOMC minutes from the April 2026 meeting. The Bureau of Economic Analysis released the March 2026 PCE showing headline inflation at three-point-five percent year over year, up from two-point-eight percent in February, with core PCE at three-point-two percent, largely driven by energy costs tied to the Middle East conflict. For bank treasurers, this means ALM duration assumptions built around two or three Fed cuts this year must be rebuilt around a flat-to-upward rate scenario extending well into 2027.
### 2. Commercial Loan Growth Offsets Consumer Softness **[Evolving]**
At Regions, CFO Anil Chadha told analysts that "approximately half of loan growth was driven by higher line utilization, with the remainder from new loan originations, primarily to existing clients," underscoring a return to activity among established corporate relationships. U.S. Bancorp highlighted sustained fee-based growth tied to merchant services, small business cards, and embedded payment tools, while KeyCorp reported a twelve percent increase in priority fee-based businesses including commercial payments and investment banking. Controllers and FP&A teams need granular line-utilization forecasting built into their NII models.
### 3. Agentic AI Moves from Pilot to Governance Test **[New]**
Looking ahead to 2026, agentic AI use in finance is poised to deliver significant short-term gains for banks, with agents serving as "always-on" relationship managers negotiating personalized products in real time while balancing customer preferences with bank risk and regulatory constraints. The sharper question is governance: the win condition is not autonomous finance but finance that compounds the controls, and a modest set of well-instrumented agents that strengthen the audit trail beats a beautiful demo of autonomous close that the auditor refuses to sign off on.
---
## Banking Finance-Function
The net interest margin outlook for 2026, given stable rates and continued deposit repricing, is generally for modest further expansion or flat margins, and 2026 may be the first year since the rate-hike cycle where year-over-year NIM comparisons are relatively flat. As of early 2026, the U.S. benchmark rate stands at three-point-seven-five percent.
On the credit side, TransUnion projects that credit card delinquency rates are expected to remain virtually flat, with the ninety-plus-day past-due rate forecast to rise by just one basis point to two-point-five-seven percent in 2026. Private credit disclosure inconsistency is an emerging controller headache: first-quarter bank reports exposed wide variation between standard metrics and banks' own private credit figures, with PNC reporting thirty-three billion dollars of business credit intermediary loans but only seven billion dollars labeled as private credit, and Citizens Financial having five-point-four billion in BCIs versus four-point-one billion in private credit.
KeyCorp's June 2026 investor presentation highlighted that the bank freed up more than four hundred million dollars in expenses, reduced risk-weighted assets by fourteen billion dollars, and described improvements to ALM governance and balance sheet resiliency to interest-rate fluctuations.
---
## Regulatory Radar
**Basel III Endgame (Active):** After nearly three years of controversy, on March 19, 2026, the Fed, OCC, and FDIC issued three proposals that would comprehensively overhaul the existing U.S. bank capital framework and implement the Basel Committee's 2017 revisions. The agencies anticipate that overall capital in the banking system "would modestly decrease" if the proposals are implemented, and the Fed voted six-to-one to advance all three proposals, with Governor Michael Barr as the sole dissenting vote. Comment deadline: June 18, 2026. Although the proposals may relieve certain capital pressures, the 2026 capital rules also add complexity and will require careful implementation, with banks needing to reassess internal models, governance frameworks, and capital planning processes.
**DFAST 2026:** The OCC stress testing rule requires covered institutions to publish a summary of stress test results in the period starting June 15 and ending July 15. Results season begins in twelve days. The 2026 global market shock scenario is characterized by heightened market expectations of persistently high inflation, higher commodity prices, and a global recession — a scenario that now has uncomfortable parallels with the real macro environment.
**Stress Test Transparency:** The Federal Reserve has requested comment on proposals to enhance the transparency and public accountability of its annual stress test, finalized hypothetical scenarios for the 2026 exercise, and voted to maintain the current stress-test-related capital requirements until public feedback can be considered.
---
## AI in Finance
**Vendor landscape:** OneStream was named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Financial Close and Consolidation Solutions. OneStream leads the market with Sensible AI embedded directly in its unified platform, while tools like Anaplan, BlackLine, and Workday Adaptive Planning offer strong capabilities in specific areas such as planning or financial close. According to Gartner 2026, sixty-two percent of cloud ERP spending will go to AI-enabled solutions by 2027, up from just fourteen percent in 2024.
**Deployment reality check:** The FP&A Trends Survey 2025 reveals that fifty-three percent of organizations still do not use AI in any FP&A process. According to Bain's 2026 CFO Survey, results to date are strongest in transactional finance, especially invoice to cash and procure to pay, even as near-term investment attention shifts toward FP&A and financial reporting.
**Agentic AI and governance:** A "finance agent owner" role is now emerging in mature agentic AI programs, typically sitting under the controller or FP&A lead, responsible for prompt curation, model-update review, and the evidence pipeline. This role does not exist in traditional finance org charts, and teams that try to absorb the work into existing roles often see it deprioritized under cycle pressure.
**Workforce signal:** 2026 will be a pivotal year for AI-powered FP&A as AI agents automate routine processes and complex analytical workflows, real-time data replaces batch updates enabling continuous forecasting, planning platforms evolve into intelligent ecosystems, and FP&A roles shift from data wrangling to orchestration, storytelling, and strategic influence.
---
## CFO Agenda, FP&A, and Transformation Signals
If 2025 was the year experimentation with AI spiked, 2026 is the year finance leaders turn that energy into operating reality, with pressure even higher for faster cycles, sharper insight, lower risk, and the ability to adapt quickly. The clearest failure mode: workflow debt happens when AI gets layered on top of existing ways of working instead of providing the impetus to change them, and if workflow debt is not addressed, AI and automation can multiply complexity instead of productivity.
Super-regional giants PNC and Truist are maintaining steady growth rates in the twelve to thirteen percent range. PNC continues to be seen as the industry's gold standard for stability leveraging its national footprint for middle-market lending, while Truist, following the multi-billion-dollar sale of its insurance division, is focused on a strategic reset.
---
## Contrarian Insight
The consensus view is that easing Basel III Endgame capital requirements is a gift to bank balance sheets. The counter-argument: although the proposals may relieve certain capital pressures, the 2026 capital rules add complexity and require careful implementation, with banks needing to reassess internal models, governance frameworks, and capital planning processes — which means the actual cost of compliance in finance and risk systems may exceed the capital relief, particularly for super-regionals building new internal model governance from scratch.
---
## Client Conversation Hooks
1. **June 18 Basel comment deadline:** Your client's capital planning team has fifteen days to formally respond to the re-proposed rules. Have they stress-tested the operational risk model implications and identified where the standardized approach diverges from their internal view of risk?
2. **Workflow debt before agentic AI:** Bain's survey shows only twelve percent of finance organizations have ML fully deployed in FP&A at scale, and most are running dual processes in parallel. The question for your client is not which AI vendor to select but whether the underlying close-to-forecast workflow is clean enough for an agent to operate in it without creating audit exposure.
3. **Private credit disclosure gap:** With PNC and Citizens showing material divergence between BCI loan reporting and private credit labels in Q1, regulators and investors are scrutinizing disclosure consistency. Is your client's controller function prepared for a formal disclosure framework on private credit exposure?
item.itunes:summary:
Now I have strong signal across all required coverage areas. Let me compile both outputs.
---
# Finance Pulse | Wednesday, June 3, 2026
**Bottom line: With the Fed locked at three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent and geopolitical inflation risks tilting the next move toward a hike, super-regional bank CFOs face a higher-for-longer rate world that is simultaneously protecting NIM stability, pressuring credit-line utilization modeling, and compressing the timeline for Basel III Endgame comment responses due June 18.**
---
## Top Takeaways
1. The Fed held the federal funds rate unchanged at the three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent target range for a third consecutive meeting in April 2026. BofA Global Research now expects the Fed to remain on hold for the rest of this year, with two quarter-point cuts pushed all the way to July and September 2027.
2. The FDIC, OCC, and Federal Reserve released proposed regulations to revise U.S. bank capital rules that would generally ease capital requirements, with comments on all three proposals due by June 18, 2026.
3. First-quarter earnings from PNC, KeyCorp, U.S. Bancorp, Truist, Fifth Third, and Regions show commercial lending gaining momentum, fee-based businesses carrying more weight, and technology investments beginning to reshape how banks compete for client relationships.
4. Bain research finds that roughly twelve percent of finance organizations have deployed machine learning in FP&A forecasting at full scale. Yet in many cases, the underlying process has not changed, with AI-generated forecasts running alongside existing bottom-up planning cycles: two processes running in parallel, neither fully trusted.
5. BlackLine CEO Owen Ryan reported accelerating revenue growth in Q1 2026 and declared that the company is "defining the future of the financial close with Agentic Financial Operations," with the growing adoption of Verity AI and Studio360 demonstrating that CFOs view BlackLine as the "essential governance layer for the AI era."
---
## Key Themes
### 1. Rate Plateau Becomes the Planning Baseline **[Recurring]**
A majority of Fed officials highlighted that some policy firming would likely become appropriate if inflation were to continue to run persistently above two percent, according to the FOMC minutes from the April 2026 meeting. The Bureau of Economic Analysis released the March 2026 PCE showing headline inflation at three-point-five percent year over year, up from two-point-eight percent in February, with core PCE at three-point-two percent, largely driven by energy costs tied to the Middle East conflict. For bank treasurers, this means ALM duration assumptions built around two or three Fed cuts this year must be rebuilt around a flat-to-upward rate scenario extending well into 2027.
### 2. Commercial Loan Growth Offsets Consumer Softness **[Evolving]**
At Regions, CFO Anil Chadha told analysts that "approximately half of loan growth was driven by higher line utilization, with the remainder from new loan originations, primarily to existing clients," underscoring a return to activity among established corporate relationships. U.S. Bancorp highlighted sustained fee-based growth tied to merchant services, small business cards, and embedded payment tools, while KeyCorp reported a twelve percent increase in priority fee-based businesses including commercial payments and investment banking. Controllers and FP&A teams need granular line-utilization forecasting built into their NII models.
### 3. Agentic AI Moves from Pilot to Governance Test **[New]**
Looking ahead to 2026, agentic AI use in finance is poised to deliver significant short-term gains for banks, with agents serving as "always-on" relationship managers negotiating personalized products in real time while balancing customer preferences with bank risk and regulatory constraints. The sharper question is governance: the win condition is not autonomous finance but finance that compounds the controls, and a modest set of well-instrumented agents that strengthen the audit trail beats a beautiful demo of autonomous close that the auditor refuses to sign off on.
---
## Banking Finance-Function
The net interest margin outlook for 2026, given stable rates and continued deposit repricing, is generally for modest further expansion or flat margins, and 2026 may be the first year since the rate-hike cycle where year-over-year NIM comparisons are relatively flat. As of early 2026, the U.S. benchmark rate stands at three-point-seven-five percent.
On the credit side, TransUnion projects that credit card delinquency rates are expected to remain virtually flat, with the ninety-plus-day past-due rate forecast to rise by just one basis point to two-point-five-seven percent in 2026. Private credit disclosure inconsistency is an emerging controller headache: first-quarter bank reports exposed wide variation between standard metrics and banks' own private credit figures, with PNC reporting thirty-three billion dollars of business credit intermediary loans but only seven billion dollars labeled as private credit, and Citizens Financial having five-point-four billion in BCIs versus four-point-one billion in private credit.
KeyCorp's June 2026 investor presentation highlighted that the bank freed up more than four hundred million dollars in expenses, reduced risk-weighted assets by fourteen billion dollars, and described improvements to ALM governance and balance sheet resiliency to interest-rate fluctuations.
---
## Regulatory Radar
**Basel III Endgame (Active):** After nearly three years of controversy, on March 19, 2026, the Fed, OCC, and FDIC issued three proposals that would comprehensively overhaul the existing U.S. bank capital framework and implement the Basel Committee's 2017 revisions. The agencies anticipate that overall capital in the banking system "would modestly decrease" if the proposals are implemented, and the Fed voted six-to-one to advance all three proposals, with Governor Michael Barr as the sole dissenting vote. Comment deadline: June 18, 2026. Although the proposals may relieve certain capital pressures, the 2026 capital rules also add complexity and will require careful implementation, with banks needing to reassess internal models, governance frameworks, and capital planning processes.
**DFAST 2026:** The OCC stress testing rule requires covered institutions to publish a summary of stress test results in the period starting June 15 and ending July 15. Results season begins in twelve days. The 2026 global market shock scenario is characterized by heightened market expectations of persistently high inflation, higher commodity prices, and a global recession — a scenario that now has uncomfortable parallels with the real macro environment.
**Stress Test Transparency:** The Federal Reserve has requested comment on proposals to enhance the transparency and public accountability of its annual stress test, finalized hypothetical scenarios for the 2026 exercise, and voted to maintain the current stress-test-related capital requirements until public feedback can be considered.
---
## AI in Finance
**Vendor landscape:** OneStream was named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Financial Close and Consolidation Solutions. OneStream leads the market with Sensible AI embedded directly in its unified platform, while tools like Anaplan, BlackLine, and Workday Adaptive Planning offer strong capabilities in specific areas such as planning or financial close. According to Gartner 2026, sixty-two percent of cloud ERP spending will go to AI-enabled solutions by 2027, up from just fourteen percent in 2024.
**Deployment reality check:** The FP&A Trends Survey 2025 reveals that fifty-three percent of organizations still do not use AI in any FP&A process. According to Bain's 2026 CFO Survey, results to date are strongest in transactional finance, especially invoice to cash and procure to pay, even as near-term investment attention shifts toward FP&A and financial reporting.
**Agentic AI and governance:** A "finance agent owner" role is now emerging in mature agentic AI programs, typically sitting under the controller or FP&A lead, responsible for prompt curation, model-update review, and the evidence pipeline. This role does not exist in traditional finance org charts, and teams that try to absorb the work into existing roles often see it deprioritized under cycle pressure.
**Workforce signal:** 2026 will be a pivotal year for AI-powered FP&A as AI agents automate routine processes and complex analytical workflows, real-time data replaces batch updates enabling continuous forecasting, planning platforms evolve into intelligent ecosystems, and FP&A roles shift from data wrangling to orchestration, storytelling, and strategic influence.
---
## CFO Agenda, FP&A, and Transformation Signals
If 2025 was the year experimentation with AI spiked, 2026 is the year finance leaders turn that energy into operating reality, with pressure even higher for faster cycles, sharper insight, lower risk, and the ability to adapt quickly. The clearest failure mode: workflow debt happens when AI gets layered on top of existing ways of working instead of providing the impetus to change them, and if workflow debt is not addressed, AI and automation can multiply complexity instead of productivity.
Super-regional giants PNC and Truist are maintaining steady growth rates in the twelve to thirteen percent range. PNC continues to be seen as the industry's gold standard for stability leveraging its national footprint for middle-market lending, while Truist, following the multi-billion-dollar sale of its insurance division, is focused on a strategic reset.
---
## Contrarian Insight
The consensus view is that easing Basel III Endgame capital requirements is a gift to bank balance sheets. The counter-argument: although the proposals may relieve certain capital pressures, the 2026 capital rules add complexity and require careful implementation, with banks needing to reassess internal models, governance frameworks, and capital planning processes — which means the actual cost of compliance in finance and risk systems may exceed the capital relief, particularly for super-regionals building new internal model governance from scratch.
---
## Client Conversation Hooks
1. **June 18 Basel comment deadline:** Your client's capital planning team has fifteen days to formally respond to the re-proposed rules. Have they stress-tested the operational risk model implications and identified where the standardized approach diverges from their internal view of risk?
2. **Workflow debt before agentic AI:** Bain's survey shows only twelve percent of finance organizations have ML fully deployed in FP&A at scale, and most are running dual processes in parallel. The question for your client is not which AI vendor to select but whether the underlying close-to-forecast workflow is clean enough for an agent to operate in it without creating audit exposure.
3. **Private credit disclosure gap:** With PNC and Citizens showing material divergence between BCI loan reporting and private credit labels in Q1, regulators and investors are scrutinizing disclosure consistency. Is your client's controller function prepared for a formal disclosure framework on private credit exposure?
